use_cases.title: Demonstrating OOP Concepts
use_cases.description: Educators use BlueJ's UML-style diagrams and live object interaction to demonstrate class relationships and method calls in real time.

features: Interactive object bench and class diagrams

enrichment.short_description: BlueMail is a free, cross-platform email client from Blix Inc. that unifies unlimited email accounts from any provider into one inbox across mobile, desktop, and web.
enrichment.headquarters: Jersey City, New Jersey, United States
enrichment.seo_meta_description: BlueMail is a free email client by Blix Inc. that unifies unlimited accounts on Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, and Linux with team and business plans.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: BlueMail is a free, cross-platform email client developed by Blix Inc., a messaging software company headquartered at 101 Hudson Street in Jersey City, New Jersey, founded in 2014 by brothers Dan and Ben Volach. Before founding Blix, the Volach brothers built Followap, an early mobile messaging pioneer that reached over 200 million subscribers at its peak and helped lay technical groundwork later reflected in messaging platforms such as WhatsApp and Facebook Messenger, before Followap was acquired by NeuStar. With BlueMail, the Volach brothers aimed to give consumers and businesses an innovative, universally compatible, and privacy-conscious email experience capable of managing an unlimited number of accounts from any email provider in one place. BlueMail is available across a wide range of platforms, including Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, Linux, and Amazon's app store, with the desktop apps bringing the same unified-inbox experience found on mobile to computers, plus browser-based webmail access. Core features include a Unified Inbox that combines all connected accounts into one view, Clusters that automatically group emails by sender or topic, Share Mail for sharing messages with family, friends, or colleagues, an Integrated Calendar for scheduling within the email app, BlueChat for built-in messaging alongside email, Gem AI for AI-assisted email composition, and a Security Center for account protection; the app also supports dark themes, push notifications, unread-mail widgets, and mobile-based printing. BlueMail attracted public attention in 2019 when Blix sued Apple in federal court in Delaware over patent infringement and antitrust allegations tied to App Store policy, after which Apple reinstated BlueMail to the App Store in February 2020 following its earlier removal. For individuals, the core BlueMail app is free to download and use. For businesses and teams, BlueMail offers paid plans layered on top of the free Standard tier: a Plus plan priced around 5 dollars per user per month that removes limitations and adds features, a Team plan priced around 12 dollars per user per month that adds an admin dashboard and priority support, and a custom-priced Enterprise plan that adds enterprise mobility management (EMM) and mobile device management (MDM) support, bring-your-own-device (BYOD) compatibility, and dedicated support for organizations with more advanced security and administration needs. Blix Inc. is estimated to employ between 51 and 200 people, while the BlueMail product team itself is reported as a much smaller group within that organization. BlueMail's target audience spans individual consumers managing several personal and work email accounts, small businesses wanting simple team email administration, and larger organizations needing centralized device and security management across a workforce that relies on mobile and desktop email. Its competitors in the email client space include Microsoft Outlook, Gmail's native app, Spark, and Edison Mail, with BlueMail differentiating itself primarily through its unlimited multi-account support, broad platform coverage including Linux, and bundled productivity extras like calendar, chat, and AI-assisted writing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is BlueMail
paragraphs: BlueMail is a free, cross-platform email client developed by Blix Inc., founded in 2014 by brothers Dan and Ben Volach and headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey. Before BlueMail, the Volach brothers built Followap, an early mobile messaging service that reached over 200 million subscribers before being acquired by NeuStar.

BlueMail is designed to unify an unlimited number of email accounts from any provider into a single inbox, and it is available on Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, Linux, and Amazon's app store, giving it broader platform coverage than many competing email clients.

In 2019, Blix sued Apple over patent infringement and antitrust claims related to App Store policy affecting BlueMail; Apple reinstated the app to the App Store in February 2020, a notable episode in BlueMail's public history.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: BlueMail's core feature is its Unified Inbox, which brings together all connected email accounts into a single, consistent view. Clusters automatically group incoming mail by sender or topic, while Share Mail lets users share messages publicly or privately with family, friends, or colleagues.

The app also includes an Integrated Calendar for scheduling directly within email, BlueChat for built-in messaging alongside email conversations, and Gem AI, a generative AI feature for composing email content. A Security Center adds account protection tools on top of standard features like dark themes, push notifications, and unread-mail widgets.

Business and team users get additional administrative capabilities on paid tiers, including a corporate management dashboard, bring-your-own-device (BYOD) support, enterprise mobility management (EMM), and remote device management for organizations that need centralized control over how employees access email.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: BlueMail's core Standard tier is free, allowing individuals to connect an unlimited number of email accounts from any provider at no cost. Paid tiers are aimed at businesses and teams that need additional administration, security, or support.

The Plus plan is priced at around 5 dollars per user per month and removes free-tier limitations, while the Team plan is priced at around 12 dollars per user per month and adds an admin dashboard along with priority support for organizations managing multiple users.

The Enterprise plan uses custom pricing and adds enterprise mobility management (EMM), mobile device management (MDM), and dedicated support for organizations with more advanced security and compliance requirements.

features.title: Unified Inbox
features.description: Combine an unlimited number of email accounts from any provider into a single inbox view.

features.title: Clusters
features.description: Automatically groups incoming emails by sender or topic for faster triage.

features.title: BlueChat
features.description: Built-in messaging that lets users chat alongside their email conversations.

features.title: Gem AI
features.description: AI-assisted email composition to help draft messages faster.

features.title: Integrated Calendar
features.description: Schedule and manage events directly within the BlueMail app.

features.title: Security Center
features.description: Centralized account protection and security controls within the app.

features.title: Share Mail
features.description: Share individual emails publicly or privately with family, friends, or colleagues.

features.title: Cross-Platform Availability
features.description: Available on Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, Linux, and Amazon's app store, plus webmail access.

pros: Free for individuals with support for unlimited email accounts from any provider
Available on nearly every major platform, including Linux
Adds productivity extras like integrated calendar, chat, and AI-assisted writing
Business tiers add admin dashboard, BYOD, and EMM/MDM support for teams
Long operating history and large installed base since its 2014 founding
cons: Some enterprise features like EMM and admin dashboards require paid or custom Enterprise plans
Historical App Store dispute with Apple (2019 to 2020) affected app availability for a period
AI features like Gem AI are newer and less established than dedicated productivity suites
Free tier functionality is more limited than the paid Plus and Team tiers

faqs.question: Is BlueMail free?
faqs.answer: Yes, BlueMail's core Standard tier is free and supports an unlimited number of connected email accounts from any provider. Paid Plus, Team, and Enterprise plans add business and admin features.

faqs.question: Who owns BlueMail?
faqs.answer: BlueMail is developed by Blix Inc., a messaging company headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey, founded in 2014 by brothers Dan and Ben Volach.

faqs.question: What platforms does BlueMail support?
faqs.answer: BlueMail is available on Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, and Linux, as well as through webmail access in a browser.

faqs.question: Does BlueMail work with Gmail and Outlook?
faqs.answer: Yes, BlueMail is designed to connect to email accounts from any provider, including Gmail, Outlook, and other IMAP or Exchange-based services.

faqs.question: How much do BlueMail paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: BlueMail's Plus plan is priced around 5 dollars per user per month and its Team plan around 12 dollars per user per month, while Enterprise uses custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does BlueMail have an AI assistant?
faqs.answer: Yes, BlueMail includes Gem AI, a generative AI feature for composing email content.

faqs.question: What is BlueMail for Teams?
faqs.answer: BlueMail for Teams refers to the paid Plus, Team, and Enterprise tiers that add features such as an admin dashboard, priority support, and EMM/MDM device management for organizations.

faqs.question: Did Apple ever remove BlueMail from the App Store?
faqs.answer: Yes, following a 2019 lawsuit between Blix and Apple over patent and antitrust claims, Apple reinstated BlueMail to the App Store in February 2020.

use_cases.title: Personal Multi-Account Email Management
use_cases.description: Individuals use BlueMail to manage multiple personal and work email accounts from different providers in one unified inbox.

use_cases.title: Small Business Team Email
use_cases.description: Small businesses use BlueMail's Team plan for shared email administration, admin dashboards, and priority support.

use_cases.title: Cross-Device Unified Inbox
use_cases.description: Users with several email providers rely on BlueMail across mobile, desktop, and web for a consistent inbox experience on every device.

enrichment.short_description: Blynk is a low-code IoT platform that lets hardware teams and hobbyists connect devices like Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi to branded mobile and web dashboards for monitoring and control.
enrichment.seo_title: Blynk: Low-Code IoT Platform for Hardware Teams
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Blynk is a low-code IoT platform for Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi, offering device connectivity, dashboards, and white-label apps from $29 per month.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Blynk began as an idea to help people build IoT mobile apps without writing code, launched publicly via a Kickstarter campaign in 2015 that quickly became one of the platform's most successful IoT crowdfunding launches and caught the attention of thousands of makers and developers worldwide. The company behind it, Blynk Inc., is now headquartered in Miami, Florida, and employs approximately 33 people across multiple continents, including Europe, Asia, and North America, with reported annual revenue around 5.5 million dollars and total funding of roughly 3.8 million dollars raised to date. Over more than a decade of operation, Blynk evolved from a hobbyist app builder into a low-code IoT platform used by more than 1.5 million developers and thousands of companies in production, spanning 136 countries and industries including smart home, agriculture, HVAC, and asset tracking; the platform reports processing over 180 billion IoT messages per month. Functionally, Blynk combines device connectivity, secure cloud infrastructure, and customizable mobile and web dashboards in one low-code system, letting hardware teams build, deploy, and manage connected products without building their own backend and app infrastructure from scratch. It natively supports Wi-Fi-enabled modules like the ESP8266 and ESP32 for direct internet connectivity, and its portable C++ Blynk Library runs on hundreds of development boards, including Arduino and Raspberry Pi, alongside broader support for over 100 device types across Wi-Fi, LoRaWAN, Cellular, Satellite, and hybrid connectivity options. Key platform capabilities include fully brandable, white-labeled iOS and Android apps; a console for managing devices, fleets, and users from one place; and, as of 2026, an AI Assistant included on all plans to help configure dashboards, debug devices, and automate workflows, with higher tiers unlocking AI-powered data conversion tools. Blynk's pricing is structured in five tiers. The Free plan costs nothing and supports up to 5 devices with one user account, one week of data retention, and roughly 100,000 messages per month, aimed at hobbyist prototyping. The Starter plan is 29 dollars per month and supports 10 devices with one user, one month of data retention, and up to 10 million messages per month, suited to hobby projects and early proofs of concept. The Prototype plan is 99 dollars per month and supports 50 devices and users with six months of data retention and unlimited messages. The Production plan ranges from roughly 199 to 1,099 dollars per month depending on device count (100 to 1,000 devices and users), and adds 12 months of data retention, unlimited messages, a 99.95 percent uptime SLA, SMS alerts, and priority support. The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and offers a white-label solution with private infrastructure, custom app publishing, 24/7 monitored private servers, on-premise deployment options, a 99.99 percent uptime SLA, and enterprise-grade security, requiring direct contact with Blynk's sales team. Blynk's target audience ranges from individual hobbyists prototyping with Arduino or ESP32 boards to startups and enterprises building commercial, white-labeled IoT products, competing with platforms such as Particle, Losant, and Ubidots in the low-code IoT space.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Blynk
paragraphs: Blynk is a low-code IoT platform that helps hardware teams and hobbyists connect physical devices to branded mobile and web dashboards for monitoring and control. It began as a Kickstarter project in 2015 aimed at letting people build IoT apps without writing code, and the campaign quickly became one of Kickstarter's most successful IoT launches.

The company behind Blynk, Blynk Inc., is headquartered in Miami, Florida, and has grown over more than a decade into a platform used by over 1.5 million developers and thousands of companies across 136 countries, processing more than 180 billion IoT messages per month.

Blynk is especially popular with the Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi hobbyist community because of its native hardware library support, while also serving startups and enterprises that need white-labeled, production-grade IoT apps.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Blynk combines device connectivity, cloud infrastructure, and a low-code dashboard builder in one platform, supporting over 100 device types across Wi-Fi, LoRaWAN, Cellular, Satellite, and hybrid connectivity. Its portable C++ Blynk Library runs on hundreds of development boards, with native support for Wi-Fi modules like the ESP8266 and ESP32.

Higher tiers of the platform add fully brandable, white-labeled iOS and Android apps, a console for managing devices, fleets, and users at scale, and role-based access controls, along with an AI Assistant included on all plans to help configure dashboards, debug devices, and automate workflows.

For businesses that need reliability guarantees, Blynk's Production and Enterprise tiers add uptime SLAs, SMS alerting, private infrastructure, and on-premise deployment options for organizations with strict data or compliance requirements.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Blynk's Free plan supports up to 5 devices with one user account, one week of data retention, and roughly 100,000 messages per month, making it a genuinely usable option for hobbyist prototyping. The Starter plan is 29 dollars per month and raises those limits to 10 devices, one month of retention, and 10 million messages per month.

The Prototype plan is 99 dollars per month for 50 devices and users with six months of retention and unlimited messages, while the Production plan ranges from about 199 to 1,099 dollars per month depending on device count (100 to 1,000 devices), adding a 99.95 percent uptime SLA, SMS alerts, and priority support.

The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and includes a fully white-labeled solution with private, 24/7-monitored infrastructure, on-premise deployment options, and a 99.99 percent uptime SLA, requiring direct contact with Blynk's sales team for a quote.

features.title: Low-Code Dashboard Builder
features.description: Build mobile and web IoT dashboards without writing custom app code.

features.title: Broad Hardware Support
features.description: Native support for Arduino, ESP32, ESP8266, Raspberry Pi, and over 100 other device types.

features.title: Multi-Protocol Connectivity
features.description: Connects devices over Wi-Fi, LoRaWAN, Cellular, Satellite, and hybrid connectivity options.

features.title: White-Label Mobile Apps
features.description: Publish fully brandable iOS and Android apps under your own company name.

features.title: Device and Fleet Management Console
features.description: Manage devices, fleets, and users from a single administrative console.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: An AI Assistant included on all plans to help configure dashboards, debug devices, and automate workflows.

features.title: Uptime SLAs and Alerts
features.description: Production and Enterprise tiers add uptime SLAs and SMS alerting for reliability-critical deployments.

features.title: Private and On-Premise Infrastructure
features.description: Enterprise tier offers private, monitored infrastructure and on-premise deployment options.

pros: Genuinely usable free tier for hobbyist prototyping with up to 5 devices
Low-code dashboard builder speeds up IoT product development
Broad hardware support including Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi
White-label branding available for commercial IoT products
Scales from hobby projects to enterprise deployments with SLA-backed production tiers
AI Assistant included on all plans, including the free tier
cons: Free and Starter tiers cap device count and message volume fairly low
Production tier pricing rises quickly with device count, from 199 to 1,099 dollars per month
Enterprise and white-label features require a custom sales conversation rather than self-serve pricing
Primarily geared toward teams building custom hardware rather than no-code end users

faqs.question: Is Blynk free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Blynk offers a free plan supporting up to 5 devices, one user account, one week of data retention, and around 100,000 messages per month.

faqs.question: What hardware does Blynk support?
faqs.answer: Blynk supports Arduino, ESP32, ESP8266, Raspberry Pi, and more than 100 other device types across Wi-Fi, LoRaWAN, Cellular, and Satellite connectivity.

faqs.question: How much does Blynk cost?
faqs.answer: Blynk's paid plans start at 29 dollars per month for the Starter tier, with Prototype at 99 dollars per month, Production ranging from 199 to 1,099 dollars per month, and custom Enterprise pricing for white-label deployments.

faqs.question: Can I white-label the Blynk app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Blynk's Production and Enterprise tiers include fully brandable, white-labeled iOS and Android apps.

faqs.question: Does Blynk require coding?
faqs.answer: Blynk is a low-code platform, so building dashboards and connecting devices requires little to no custom app coding, though device firmware still typically uses the Blynk library.

faqs.question: What is Blynk used for?
faqs.answer: Blynk is used to connect, monitor, and control IoT devices through mobile and web dashboards, spanning hobbyist projects to commercial products in smart home, agriculture, HVAC, and asset tracking.

faqs.question: Does Blynk support Raspberry Pi?
faqs.answer: Yes, Raspberry Pi is one of the development boards natively supported by the Blynk platform and its C++ library.

faqs.question: Where is Blynk headquartered?
faqs.answer: Blynk Inc. is headquartered in Miami, Florida, United States.

use_cases.title: Hobbyist Arduino and ESP32 Projects
use_cases.description: Makers use Blynk's free tier to connect Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi projects to mobile dashboards for monitoring and control.

use_cases.title: Branded IoT Product Development
use_cases.description: Startups use Blynk's white-label apps and device management console to launch commercial connected products under their own brand.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Fleet Monitoring
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use Blynk's Production and Enterprise tiers to monitor and manage large fleets of connected devices with SLA-backed reliability.

enrichment.short_description: Bold Commerce is a Winnipeg-based e-commerce technology company known for its suite of Shopify apps, including Bold Subscriptions, Bold Checkout, and Bold Upsell, plus composable commerce infrastructure for recurring revenue.
enrichment.starting_price: Free for eligible small stores; paid plans from around $24.99/month plus a transaction fee
enrichment.seo_title: Bold Commerce: Shopify Subscriptions and Checkout Apps
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bold Commerce powers Shopify apps like Bold Subscriptions, Checkout, and Upsell, plus composable commerce tools, used by over 760,000 merchants.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Bold Commerce was founded in 2012 in a basement in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, by four co-founders: Jay Myers, Stefan Maynard, Eric Boisjoli, and Yvan Boisjoli. Myers, who had been selling his family's archery products online since 1998, identified a need for better tools to improve the e-commerce experience on the then-young Shopify platform, and the team's first app, Product Upsell, quickly gained traction. All four founders initially held full-time jobs elsewhere and transitioned into Bold Commerce one by one between October 2012 and March 2013 as the business grew. The company remained bootstrapped until its first outside funding round in 2019, and later raised 35 million dollars from investors including OMERS Ventures, the venture capital arm of the Ontario Municipal Employees Retirement System, funding that supported plans to hire around 100 additional staff. Bold Commerce remains headquartered in Winnipeg, at 50 Fultz Boulevard, and employs an estimated 201 to 500 people, with staff spread across North America, Europe, and Asia. The company describes itself as 'the repeat commerce company,' focused on helping Shopify merchants increase customer lifetime value by driving repeat purchases and higher-value transactions. Its product suite centers on six core apps: Bold Subscriptions for recurring orders and subscription billing, Bold Checkout for customizable, high-conversion Shopify checkout experiences, Bold Upsell for cart and post-purchase upsell offers, Bold Discounts for promotional pricing rules, Bold Custom Pricing for tiered or negotiated pricing, Bold Memberships for gated content and membership tiers, and rePete, a reorder-nudging tool that prompts customers to repurchase consumable products. Bold holds Shopify Plus certification and describes itself as the first Shopify front-end app developer, reflecting its long tenure building on the Shopify App Store; the company reports that more than 760,000 online merchants have installed apps it has built, and that its Subscriptions app alone powers more than 41,000 subscription brands. On pricing, Bold Subscriptions offers a free option for stores generating less than 100 dollars per month in subscription revenue, plus a 90-day free trial variant for new subscription businesses; the entry paid tier costs 24.99 dollars per month plus a 2 percent transaction fee for stores generating under 2,500 dollars per month in subscription revenue, with pricing scaling up through additional tiers as subscription revenue grows, and custom Enterprise pricing available for businesses generating over 1 million dollars in annual subscription revenue. Bold's other apps, such as Checkout and Upsell, are typically priced and sold separately through the Shopify App Store rather than bundled into a single flat subscription. Bold Commerce's primary customers are Shopify and Shopify Plus merchants ranging from small direct-to-consumer brands launching their first subscription program up to larger, high-volume retailers needing a highly customizable checkout experience; its competitors include Recharge and Skio in subscriptions, and Shopify's own native checkout customization tools for merchants who do not need Bold's additional layer of functionality.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Bold Commerce
paragraphs: Bold Commerce is an e-commerce technology company founded in 2012 in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, by Jay Myers, Stefan Maynard, Eric Boisjoli, and Yvan Boisjoli. The company began by building apps to improve the Shopify merchant experience, starting with a product upsell tool, and has since grown into one of the longest-running third-party app developers on the Shopify platform.

Bold describes itself as 'the repeat commerce company,' focused on helping Shopify merchants generate more repeat purchases and higher customer lifetime value through subscriptions, upsells, discounts, and a customizable checkout. It remained bootstrapped until 2019, when it took on outside funding, including 35 million dollars backed by OMERS Ventures.

The company remains headquartered in Winnipeg and reports that more than 760,000 online merchants have installed apps it has built, making it one of the more established names in the Shopify app ecosystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Bold Commerce's core product suite includes Bold Subscriptions for recurring orders and subscription billing, Bold Checkout for a customizable and high-conversion Shopify checkout experience, and Bold Upsell for cart and post-purchase upsell offers.

Additional apps round out the suite: Bold Discounts for promotional pricing rules, Bold Custom Pricing for tiered or negotiated pricing, Bold Memberships for gated content and membership tiers, and rePete, a reorder-nudging tool that encourages customers to repurchase consumable products.

Bold holds Shopify Plus certification and positions itself as the first Shopify front-end app developer, reflecting deep, long-standing integration with the Shopify platform rather than a general-purpose e-commerce toolkit built for multiple platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Bold Subscriptions offers a free option for stores generating under 100 dollars per month in subscription revenue, along with a 90-day free trial for new subscription businesses evaluating the product. The entry paid tier is 24.99 dollars per month plus a 2 percent transaction fee for stores with under 2,500 dollars per month in subscription revenue.

Pricing scales through additional tiers as a merchant's subscription revenue grows, with custom Enterprise pricing available for businesses generating over 1 million dollars in annual subscription revenue.

Bold's other apps, such as Checkout and Upsell, are generally priced and purchased separately through the Shopify App Store rather than bundled into a single Bold Commerce subscription, so total cost depends on which combination of apps a merchant adopts.

features.title: Bold Subscriptions
features.description: Recurring order and subscription billing management for Shopify merchants.

features.title: Bold Checkout
features.description: A customizable, high-conversion checkout experience for Shopify Plus stores.

features.title: Bold Upsell
features.description: Cart and post-purchase upsell offers to increase average order value.

features.title: Bold Discounts
features.description: Promotional pricing and discount rule management for Shopify stores.

features.title: Bold Custom Pricing
features.description: Tiered or negotiated pricing tools for B2B or wholesale-style Shopify storefronts.

features.title: Bold Memberships
features.description: Gated content and membership tier management for subscription-based access.

features.title: rePete Reorder Nudging
features.description: Prompts customers to repurchase consumable products at the right time to drive repeat orders.

features.title: Shopify Plus Certification
features.description: Certified integration for high-volume Shopify Plus merchants requiring enterprise-grade reliability.

pros: Deep, long-standing integration with Shopify, dating back to Shopify's early app ecosystem
Modular apps let merchants adopt only the specific tools they need
Proven at scale, with more than 760,000 merchant installs of Bold's apps
Shopify Plus certified partner with enterprise-grade checkout extensibility
Free tier available for very small subscription businesses
cons: Full feature set is spread across multiple separate apps rather than one bundled product
Percentage-based transaction fees on subscription revenue add cost as a store scales
Enterprise and composable commerce pricing is custom and not published
Primarily built around the Shopify ecosystem, which may limit portability for merchants on other platforms

faqs.question: What does Bold Commerce do?
faqs.answer: Bold Commerce builds e-commerce apps for Shopify merchants, including Bold Subscriptions, Bold Checkout, and Bold Upsell, focused on helping brands drive repeat purchases and higher order values.

faqs.question: Is Bold Commerce free?
faqs.answer: Bold Subscriptions offers a free option for stores generating under 100 dollars per month in subscription revenue, with paid tiers starting at 24.99 dollars per month plus a transaction fee as revenue grows.

faqs.question: How much does Bold Subscriptions cost?
faqs.answer: Bold Subscriptions starts at 24.99 dollars per month plus a 2 percent transaction fee for stores under 2,500 dollars per month in subscription revenue, with custom Enterprise pricing above 1 million dollars in annual subscription revenue.

faqs.question: Does Bold Commerce work outside Shopify?
faqs.answer: Bold Commerce's primary and best-known apps are built for the Shopify and Shopify Plus ecosystem; its composable commerce platform extends some capabilities beyond Shopify for larger enterprise customers.

faqs.question: What is Bold Checkout?
faqs.answer: Bold Checkout is a customizable checkout app for Shopify Plus merchants designed to improve conversion rates during the checkout process.

faqs.question: Who founded Bold Commerce?
faqs.answer: Bold Commerce was founded in 2012 in Winnipeg, Manitoba, by Jay Myers, Stefan Maynard, Eric Boisjoli, and Yvan Boisjoli.

faqs.question: How many merchants use Bold Commerce apps?
faqs.answer: Bold Commerce reports that more than 760,000 online merchants have installed apps it has built, with Bold Subscriptions alone powering over 41,000 subscription brands.

faqs.question: Is Bold Commerce Shopify Plus certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bold Commerce is a Shopify Plus certified partner.

use_cases.title: Launching a Subscription Program
use_cases.description: Shopify merchants use Bold Subscriptions to set up recurring orders and subscription billing for consumable or replenishable products.

use_cases.title: High-Conversion Checkout for Shopify Plus
use_cases.description: Larger Shopify Plus brands use Bold Checkout to customize and optimize their checkout flow for higher conversion rates.

use_cases.title: Increasing Average Order Value
use_cases.description: DTC brands use Bold Upsell and Bold Discounts together to run cart and post-purchase upsell campaigns alongside promotional pricing.

features: 90-day free trial option for new stores
